title:
Module 8
Account Safety & Precautions
subtitle: Learn How to Protect Your Social Media Accounts from Bans and What to Do if It Happens

blocks.content: There are two types of bans: visible bans and invisible bans. Visible bans are when the platform tells you directly. Invisible bans (shadowbans) are far more dangerous because you will not know it happened until your growth flatlines. Understanding both is critical to protecting your accounts.

items: Content removal — single post taken down (warning level)
Temporary restriction — limited features for 24-72 hours
Shadowban — content stops being shown to non-followers (invisible)
Account suspension — temporary lockout requiring appeal
Permanent ban — account deleted, usually not recoverable
Posting suggestive content too early (no trust built)
Aggressive follow/unfollow behavior
Spam-like posting patterns (too much, too fast)
Link sharing during warmup phase
Multiple accounts on same device/IP behaving identically
Reports from other users

right.content: A shadowban will NOT show a notification. Your content simply stops reaching new people. The only way to detect it is by monitoring your metrics.

items: Instagram: Check Insights — if 'reach from non-followers' drops to near zero, you are likely shadowbanned
TikTok: If views suddenly drop from thousands to double digits
Twitter: Use shadowban checking tools to verify
Reddit: If your posts get zero upvotes consistently in subreddits where they used to perform

right.title: IMAGE PLACEHOLDER
right.content: Instagram Insights screenshot comparing normal reach vs shadowbanned reach

right.title: Warning
right.content: By the time you notice a shadowban, it has usually been active for days. Check your metrics daily. Early detection = faster recovery.

blocks.content: Prevention is infinitely easier than recovery. These daily habits keep your accounts healthy and your algorithmic trust high. Treat these as non-negotiable rules, not suggestions.

items: Scroll your feed for 10-15 minutes before posting anything
Like and comment on other creators' posts genuinely
Reply to every comment on your own posts (engagement signal)
Watch Stories and Reels from accounts you follow
Keep follow/unfollow activity low — max 10-15 per day
Post at consistent times each day
Never push content boundaries on Instagram or TikTok
Keep swimwear/lingerie content to minimum on strict platforms
No sexual captions, no suggestive language
If unsure whether content is safe, don't post it
Instagram is your revenue engine — protect it above everything
Never switch between accounts rapidly on the same device
Log out of personal accounts before using persona accounts
Keep VPN consistent — don't jump between locations
Don't use third-party apps that require Instagram login
Avoid automation tools that violate platform ToS
Keep your phone's OS and apps updated
Reach from non-followers (should be steady or growing)
Engagement rate per post (likes + comments / followers)
Story views (should be 10-30% of follower count)
Profile visits (should be steady or growing)
Any warning notifications from the platform

right.title: IMAGE PLACEHOLDER
right.content: Daily safety checklist graphic showing trust signals, content rules, and metrics to monitor

sections.header: Lesson 3: What to Do If You Get Banned

blocks.content: Despite best practices, bans happen. They are part of operating in this space. What separates winners from quitters is how fast and smart you respond. This lesson covers exact steps for each scenario.

items: STOP posting immediately — do not try to 'post through it'
Reduce all activity to passive scrolling only for 48-72 hours
Remove any recently posted content that may have triggered it
Check if you violated any content guidelines recently
After 48-72 hours, resume posting slowly (50% of normal frequency)
Monitor reach metrics — shadowbans typically lift in 7-14 days
Submit an appeal immediately through the platform's process
Be polite, professional, and brief in your appeal
Do not create a new account on the same device/IP while suspended
Wait for the appeal — most respond within 24-72 hours
If appeal fails, wait 30 days and try again with new evidence
Accept the loss — do not waste time on repeated appeals
Analyze what went wrong so you do not repeat it
Start fresh with a new persona (different look, name, niche)
Use a completely new device, phone number, and IP address
Do NOT try to recreate the same persona — platforms detect this
Follow the warmup protocol from Module 5 with extra caution

right.title: Recovery Timeline
right.content:
Shadowban: 7-14 days of reduced activity.
Temporary suspension: 24-72 hours for appeal.
Permanent ban: Start fresh — full warmup (21-28 days) on new device.

right.title: Mindset
right.content: A ban is not the end. It is a setback. If your system works, you can rebuild. The knowledge and content pipeline you have built does not disappear because one account does. Rebuild smarter.

blocks.content: Running multiple accounts is the highest-risk activity in this business. Platforms are designed to detect multi-account operations. One mistake can cascade and take down all your accounts simultaneously. These rules are absolute.

items: One phone per persona (ideal)
Max 2 accounts per device (absolute maximum)
TikTok: max 3 accounts per device
Never log into two personas in the same app session
Never switch between accounts rapidly
Factory reset devices between personas if reusing hardware
Each persona should use a different IP address
Use mobile data (different SIM) or separate VPN profiles
Never access two personas from the same WiFi network simultaneously
Keep VPN locations consistent per persona — don't jump around
Each persona gets a unique email address
Each persona gets a unique phone number
Never cross-follow between your own accounts
Never share links between your own accounts
Never mention one persona from another
Different content niches and posting schedules
Same IP address across accounts
Same device fingerprint
Cross-following between your own accounts
Identical posting patterns (same times, same hashtags)
Shared link in bio URLs
Logging into multiple accounts from one device in rapid succession

right.title: Nuclear Rule
right.content: If one account gets banned, do NOT access your other accounts from the same device or IP for at least 72 hours. A ban investigation can cascade to linked accounts. Quarantine immediately.

summary_points: Shadowbans are invisible — monitor your reach metrics daily to detect them early
Prevention beats recovery: daily trust signals and safe content keep accounts healthy
If shadowbanned: stop posting, go passive for 48-72 hours, resume at 50% frequency
If permanently banned: start fresh with new device, new persona, new everything
Multi-account safety requires complete separation: device, IP, email, phone number
If one account gets banned, quarantine all other accounts for 72 hours minimum
